title: Provide a skip-to-content link
summary: AI-generated interfaces lack skip-navigation links, forcing keyboard users to tab through every navigation item before reaching main content.
instruction: Include a visible-on-focus skip-to-content link as the first focusable element on every page. The link must become visible when focused via keyboard navigation.
rationale: WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) requires a mechanism to skip repeated content. On a page with 10 navigation links, a keyboard user must tab through every one on every page visit. The standard solution is a skip link, which benefits screen reader users, keyboard navigators, and power users.

violations: A marketing page with 8 nav links plus search before main content and no skip link.
A dashboard with a 15-item sidebar and no skip-link at the top of the page.
controls: A page with Skip to content as the first focusable element, visible on focus.
A page with a skip-link visually hidden via clip/position but visible on keyboard focus.
borderline: Single-page applications where content regions load dynamically - the skip link should target the content container that updates.

exceptions.description: Pages with no repeated navigation blocks (single-content landing pages) and screen-reader-only applications where navigation order is managed by ARIA landmarks.
examples: A single-section landing page with no navigation
An ARIA-managed SPA with proper landmark navigation

detector.signalDescription: Check if the first focusable element in the DOM is a skip-link (anchor with href targeting a main-content region, or a button with skip text). Verify a target main element or id='content' exists.
limitations: Skip links added by JavaScript after page load may not appear in static DOM.
Some frameworks manage focus programmatically, making skip links unnecessary.

scenarios: Pages with no navigation (single-content landing pages where there's nothing to skip).
SPA frameworks that programmatically manage focus on route change.
mitigations: Check for navigation blocks before flagging. Accept framework-based focus management as an alternative approach.

fixturePlan.description: A page with 10 navigation links followed by main content, no skip link present.
Same page with a visible-on-focus skip-to-content link as the first element after body.
An SPA with no skip link but programmatic focus management on route navigation.

notes: Normalization revision: impact and detection verified against WCAG 2.4.1. Source reference confirmed. Detector correctly scoped to DOM structure check.
